---
title: "Past Years"
format: html
editor: visual
---

Pictures from last year!

![Observation event with the Conservation Biology class at NMU.](images/2025/2025_images/Copy%20of%20Marquette_Michigan_USA_OwenVanAntwerpen.JPG){fig-align="center" width="160"}

![Observation event at the Presque Isle Bog.](images/2025/2025_images/bog.JPG){fig-align="center" width="160"}

![Observation event at Harlow Lake with the Marquette County Conservation District.](images/2025/2025_images/macro.JPG){fig-align="center" width="160"}

```{r}
#| label: inat_barchart
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)

mqt <- read_csv("graphs/mqt_inat.csv") |>
  filter(quality_grade != "casual") |>
   group_by(iconic_taxon_name) |> 
  summarise(mqt_count = n()) |>
  na.omit() |>
  arrange(desc(mqt_count)) #add that I made it and got data from iNat + titles & captions

ggplot(mqt, aes(x = reorder(iconic_taxon_name, -mqt_count), y = mqt_count, fill = iconic_taxon_name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Counts of Taxa from the 2025 City Nature Challenge",
    x = "Taxon",
    y = "Count",
    caption = "Figure created by Mattea Muscat using data from iNaturalist"
  ) + 
  coord_flip()

```

```{r}

#| label: interactive_map
#| fig-cap: "Observations of species in Marquette County (zoom in and click on observations to see the species name)"
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false




library(sf)
library(readr)
library(leaflet)

# read the csv as a tibble (tidyverse dataframe)
mqt <- read_csv('graphs/mqt_inat.csv')

# convert the tibble/dataframe to an sf (spatial feature) object with the
# coords being the lat/long column and the crs defined
mqt_sf <- st_as_sf(mqt, 
                   coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), 
                   crs = 4326)

# note you have to manually create a color palette. leaflet will not generate
# one for you. the palette argument refers to a predefined color palette
# see here for more info on this function:
# https://rstudio.github.io/leaflet/reference/colorNumeric.html
pal <- colorFactor(palette = "Set1", domain = mqt_sf$iconic_taxon_name)

# pass the sf object which has a geometry column to leaflet
# we need to specify to add circleMarkers for it to plot points
# for more info go here:
# https://rstudio.github.io/leaflet/articles/markers.html#circle-markers
leaflet(mqt_sf) |>
  addTiles() |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    radius = 5,
    # pass the color palette to the color argument
    color = ~pal(iconic_taxon_name),
    stroke = FALSE, 
    fillOpacity = 0.7,
    popup = ~paste("Species:", common_name)
)
```
